description: Canonical operator control-loop board: reconcile/admission throughput, turn-submit dispatch, agent wrapper HTTP, webhooks, SCM writes/writeback, REST API, scan/triage/backlog, push-CD cascades, systemic grouping, and memory/lifecycle faults. Error-ratio and threshold panels mirror the exact PromQL of the matching rule in alerts/tatara-operator.yaml so the number on-call sees here equals the number that fired. Replaces operator-health.json, operator-workflows.json and tatara-operator.json (merged and deduped).

panels.description: Turn-submit throughput broken out by Task kind and result. result=ok is a successful dispatch, result=transient is a benign requeue (boot-race / 503 / 425 / 409 session-busy backpressure), result=error is a hard failure that backs off the reconcile. Fine-grained transport causes live on the Agent Wrapper HTTP row.
panels.title: Turn-submit rate by kind and result

panels.description: Fraction of turn submits that hard-errored over the last 15m, gated to >=10 submissions. Exact PromQL of the 'Operator turn submit failure ratio high' rule in alerts/tatara-operator.yaml (clamp_min avoids divide-by-zero on an idle operator). transient requeues are intentionally excluded.
panels.title: Turn-submit error ratio (alert: Operator turn submit failure ratio high)

panels.description: p95 wall-clock of SubmitTurn calls, broken out by Task kind. Red line at 30s mirrors the 'Operator turn submit p95 latency high' alert (which uses the aggregate p95 across all kinds).
panels.title: Turn-submit p95 duration by kind

panels.description: Operator-to-wrapper HTTP call rate by method and outcome. outcome is ok, http_error (non-2xx response), transport_error (connection failure) or unreachable (no route to the pod).
panels.title: Agent HTTP rate by method and outcome

panels.description: Rate of transport-level agent HTTP failures (unreachable / timeout / transport_error) over 10m. Exact PromQL of the 'Operator agent HTTP failure spike' rule in alerts/tatara-operator.yaml.
panels.title: Agent HTTP transport errors (alert: Operator agent HTTP failure spike)

panels.description: p95 wall-clock of operator-to-wrapper HTTP calls by method.
panels.title: Agent HTTP p95 duration by method

panels.description: Inbound webhook event rate by provider and result. provider=github|gitlab; result includes ok, error, bad_signature, bad_request and the benign accepted/created/duplicate/ignored/reactivated/task_created outcomes.
panels.title: Webhook event rate by provider and result

panels.description: Fraction of webhook events that were error/bad_signature/bad_request over 15m. Exact PromQL of the 'Operator webhook error ratio high' rule in alerts/tatara-operator.yaml (clamp_min avoids idle divide-by-zero).
panels.title: Webhook error ratio (alert: Operator webhook error ratio high)

panels.description: p95 webhook handling wall-clock by provider. Slow apiserver/secret lookups surface here before GitHub's 10s delivery timeout.
panels.title: Webhook p95 handling duration by provider

panels.description: SCM write operation rate by verb and result, filtered to kind=write (excludes read-only verbs like list_open_issues so the mix is not diluted by read traffic). result=ok succeeded, result=blocked was refused by an egress guard (benign), result=error is a genuine failure the error-ratio alert watches.
panels.title: SCM write rate by verb and result

panels.description: Fraction of SCM operations that errored over 15m. Exact PromQL of the 'Operator SCM write failure ratio high' rule in alerts/tatara-operator.yaml (clamp_min avoids idle divide-by-zero). Deliberately unfiltered by kind to match the alert's own aggregate.
panels.title: SCM error ratio (alert: Operator SCM write failure ratio high)

panels.description: SCM operations that errored, keyed by provider and classified HTTP status (or network for connect/timeout). Names WHICH status is driving the SCM error ratio.
panels.title: SCM request errors by provider and status

panels.description: Writeback terminal outcomes by result. opened is a real PR/MR; no_change/no_branch/no_pr/in_scope_no_branch are benign no-ops; skip_4xx / skip_4xx_capped are the permanent-4xx skips broken down on the panel below.
panels.title: Writeback outcomes by result

panels.description: Writeback repo-skips on a permanent 4xx over the alert's 30m window. Series A is the aggregate skip_4xx count that the 'Operator writeback 404 loop' rule (alerts/tatara-operator.yaml) thresholds; series B breaks it down by HTTP status and reason (already_exists|other) so the failure mode is named without pulling the leader pod logs.
panels.title: Writeback 4xx skips by status/reason (alert: Operator writeback 404 loop)

panels.description: Operator REST API request rate by handler endpoint and result.
panels.title: REST API request rate by endpoint and result

panels.description: p95 REST API handler execution wall-clock by endpoint.
panels.title: REST API p95 duration by endpoint

panels.description: Reconcile throughput by CRD kind. result=ok|error|transient; this panel counts all outcomes.
panels.title: Reconcile rate by kind

panels.description: Aggregate reconcile error ratio (result=transient excluded, matching CONVENTIONS.md). Exact PromQL of the 'Operator reconcile error ratio high' rule in alerts/tatara-operator.yaml so the number here equals the number that paged.
panels.title: Reconcile error ratio (alert: Operator reconcile error ratio high)

panels.description: Tasks reaching a terminal phase by phase and kind. phase=Succeeded|Failed. Backs the 'Operator task failure spike' alert (>5 Tasks Failed in 1h).
panels.title: Task terminal rate by phase and kind

panels.description: Queued (not-yet-admitted) QueuedEvents per project and pool class. Leader-only gauge; max() collapses non-leader replicas. Red line at 10 mirrors the 'Operator queue depth backlog' alert (events backing up faster than they admit).
panels.title: Queue depth by project and class (alert: Operator queue depth backlog)

panels.description: Admitted in-flight QueuedEvents per project and pool class. Leader-only gauge; max() collapses non-leader replicas. Read alongside queue depth: high inflight + high depth means the lane is saturated (see the 'Operator tasks inflight pinned at cap' alert).
panels.title: Queue inflight by project and class

panels.description: Rate at which QueuedEvents are admitted into flight, split by pool class and Task kind. This is the throughput of the admission gate; a drop to zero while queue depth is non-zero means admission is stuck.
panels.title: Admission rate by class and kind

panels.description: Rate of admission declines by project, pool class and reason. reason=token_budget today. This is a 0-series until a project enables a token-budget window (issue #189); an empty panel means nothing has been declined in-window, not a broken query.
panels.title: Admission declines by reason (wired-but-quiet)

panels.description: Rate of scanned items per scan activity (issueScan, mrScan, brainstorm, backstop) split by outcome (picked, scanned, skipped_dedup, skipped_cap, skipped_scope, ...). Shows how much each scan loop churns through and what fraction it acts on versus skips.
panels.title: Scan items by activity and outcome

panels.description: Rate of Tasks created out of each scan activity, split by Task kind. This is the conversion of scanned work into actual agent Tasks (the productive output of the scan loops).
panels.title: Tasks created by activity and kind

panels.description: p95 scan duration per scan activity. The per-activity idle guard (and on the sample count > 0) suppresses NaN spikes from an activity with no samples in-window, per CONVENTIONS.md section 4.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, activity) (rate(tatara_scan_duration_seconds_bucket[15m]))) and (sum by (activity) (rate(tatara_scan_duration_seconds_count[15m])) > 0)

panels.title: Scan duration p95 by activity

panels.description: Rate of triage outcomes by action (implement, discuss, close, close-withheld). The mix shows what the operator decides to do with issues it triages.
panels.title: Triage outcomes by action

panels.description: Open bot-authored proposals per repo (leader-only gauge; max() collapses non-leader replicas). This is the standing backlog the brainstorm loop has generated and not yet had closed/declined. Watched against the project-wide maxOpenProposals cap.
panels.title: Open proposals by repo

panels.description: Rate of brainstorm-loop outcomes by result (proposed = a new proposal issue was opened; no_yield = the loop ran but produced nothing). The proposed rate is the backlog-growth driver.
panels.title: Brainstorm outcomes by result

panels.description: Number of semver push-CD cascades in a FAILED state (a deploy stage broke and bounded recovery has not cleared it). Merged component code is not reaching the cluster while this is > 0. The 'or vector(0)' renders 0 (not No Data) when no cascade series exist. Mirrors the 'Tatara CD cascade failed' alert.
panels.title: CD cascades failed

panels.description: Number of semver push-CD cascades STALLED past the Deploying deadline with no live watcher (cdScan backstop trip). The deploy train is wedged while this is > 0. The 'or vector(0)' renders 0 (not No Data) when no cascade series exist. Mirrors the 'Tatara CD cascade stalled' alert.
panels.title: CD cascades stalled

panels.description: Rate of push-CD cascades that resolved (a merged component change fully cascaded to a tatara-helmfile apply). This is the delivered-to-cluster throughput of the deploy path. Leader-only counter (no project label); summed across replicas.
panels.title: CD resolved throughput

panels.description: Sibling issues collapsed into a systemic-issue lead over the last 24h (dedup savings from lead-per-repo election). Currently a dark/0-series counter; the 'or vector(0)' renders 0 (not No Data) until the first collapse.
panels.title: Systemic siblings collapsed (24h)

panels.description: Systemic groups elected (a lead issue got a combined-PR agent) over the last 24h. Uses the same 24h increase() window as the sibling-collapsed panel above, since the underlying counter is monotonic and a raw max() would show cumulative-since-start rather than recent activity. The 'or vector(0)' renders 0 (not No Data) until the first group is led.
panels.title: Systemic groups led (24h)

panels.description: Per-project tatara-memory stacks by phase. phase=Failed>0 is critical (agents for that project cannot read/write memory); see the 'Operator memory stack failed' alert.
panels.title: Memory stacks by phase (alert: Operator memory stack failed)

panels.description: Per-project memory-stack provisioning p95: time for a fresh project's memory stack to reach Ready. Gaps mean no provisioning happened in-window.
panels.title: Memory provision p95

panels.description: Agent-pod lifecycle fault rates: boot-crash (operator_agent_boot_crash_total{outcome=failed}), unreachable-termination (operator_agent_unreachable_termination_total), and orphan-reaped (operator_orphan_reaped_total). Backs the 'Operator agent boot crash budget exhausted' and 'Operator agent unreachable terminations' alerts. NOTE: boot_crash is wired-but-quiet - it reads 0 until a wrapper Pod exhausts the boot-respawn budget, so a flat zero line here is healthy.
panels.title: Agent lifecycle faults

title: Tatara - Operator
